channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Family, former presidents and a Hall of Famer give Rev. Jesse Jackson a final sendoff
item.description: Several speakers at Jackson's funeral invoked his hallmark catchphrases: "Keep hope alive" and "I am somebody."
item.content:encoded:
Several speakers at Jackson's funeral invoked his hallmark catchphrases: "Keep hope alive" and "I am somebody."

(Image credit: Erin Hooley)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Bernard LaFayette, Selma voting rights organizer, dies at 85
item.description: Bernard LaFayette, who died Thursday, laid the foundations of the Selma, Alabama, campaign that culminated in the passage of the Voting Rights Act. He was a Freedom Rider and helped found the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee.
item.content:encoded:
Bernard LaFayette, who died Thursday, laid the foundations of the Selma, Alabama, campaign that culminated in the passage of the Voting Rights Act. He was a Freedom Rider and helped found the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee.

(Image credit: Gregory Smith)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Political and religious leaders celebrate Jesse Jackson's life at 'homegoing' service
item.description: A celebration of the life of civil rights leader Jesse Jackson will be held in Chicago Friday. Current and former elected officials, faith leaders, entertainers and more are expected to attend. A private funeral will be held Saturday.
item.content:encoded: A celebration of the life of civil rights leader Jesse Jackson will be held in Chicago Friday. Current and former elected officials, faith leaders, entertainers and more are expected to attend. A private funeral will be held Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Cheryl Corley

item.title: General Motors makes its debut and Ford returns to Formula One racing
item.description: The Formula 1 World Championship racing season begins this weekend. It's the most expensive, technically advanced version of auto racing, and both Ford and General Motors will be making history.
item.content:encoded: The Formula 1 World Championship racing season begins this weekend. It's the most expensive, technically advanced version of auto racing, and both Ford and General Motors will be making history.
item.dc:creator: Quinn Klinefelter

item.title: Oil surges to its highest price since 2023, and stocks drop after U.S. jobs report
item.description: Oil shot to its highest price since 2023 after surging again because of the Iran war, and a weak update on the U.S. job market knocked stocks lower to cap Wall Street's worst week since October.
item.content:encoded:
Oil shot to its highest price since 2023 after surging again because of the Iran war, and a weak update on the U.S. job market knocked stocks lower to cap Wall Street's worst week since October.

(Image credit: Seth Wenig)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Your Winter Paralympics primer: What, who and how to watch
item.description: Hundreds of para athletes are competing in Italy through March 15. Many Americans are defending past titles, with the U.S. sled hockey team hoping to fend off rival Canada for its fifth straight gold.
item.content:encoded:
Hundreds of para athletes are competing in Italy through March 15. Many Americans are defending past titles, with the U.S. sled hockey team hoping to fend off rival Canada for its fifth straight gold.

(Image credit: Mattia Ozbot)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Trump fires Kristi Noem. And, DOJ releases some missing Epstein files
item.description: President Trump has fired DHS Secretary Kristi Noem and named Sen. Markwayne Mullin as her replacement. And, the Justice Department released some missing Epstein files.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump has fired DHS Secretary Kristi Noem and named Sen. Markwayne Mullin as her replacement. And, the Justice Department released some missing Epstein files.

(Image credit: Mandel Ngan, Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Was that really Jim Carrey? The internet had thoughts but the quiz has answers
item.content:encoded: Plus: Primates of all varieties!
item.dc:creator: Holly J. Morris

item.title: Watch live: Homegoing service celebrates civil rights leader Jesse Jackson in Chicago
item.description: Former Presidents Barack Obama and Bill Clinton are speaking at the event, which also features music from Chicago native Jennifer Hudson. Other attendees include Sen. Dick Durbin and Mr. T.
item.content:encoded:
Former Presidents Barack Obama and Bill Clinton are speaking at the event, which also features music from Chicago native Jennifer Hudson. Other attendees include Sen. Dick Durbin and Mr. T.

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Members of the public voice their disapproval of Trump's ballroom plan at hearing
item.description: The panel tasked with approving President Trump's ballroom heard from citizens Thursday who are overwhelmingly opposed to the president's plan.
item.content:encoded: The panel tasked with approving President Trump's ballroom heard from citizens Thursday who are overwhelmingly opposed to the president's plan.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith
